page: Microsoft Azure E64as_v7 is an Easv7 family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is allocated 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for several workloads. It achieves top-tier status in single-core CPU performance via stress-ng, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, both cached and uncached, rank in the top 25 percent. In database operations, the server scores in the top 10 percent for PassMark database operations and demonstrates strong performance with 98.7 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Large-model LLM inference drops to average tiers, while smaller model inference speeds remain in the top 10 to 25 percent.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per vCPU and lack of bundled local storage, the E64as_v7 offers a specialized hardware profile optimized for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of local NVMe drives. This resource density makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications that require substantial RAM but rely on external Premium Storage. It is also suited for memory-bound data processing, medium-sized language model inference, and high-throughput web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64as_v7 is an Easv7 family virtual machine configured with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, this instance features 32 physical cores and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution, database operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads. It is suited for large-scale database management, in-memory caching, and data processing applications that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v7 is an Easv7 virtual machine with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It delivers top-tier database and single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v7 features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-memory virtual machine featuring sixty-four virtual cores and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding database and enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
Equipped with 512 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier performance in database operations and single-core CPU benchmarks
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or bundled GPUs
Optimized for in-memory caching, large databases, and enterprise applications
